rationale: Both candidates independently verify the same core facts as the ground-truth inline comment (submit_initial/submit_update reject lists for diffusion, so the already_submitted gate was spurious) and catch the DiffusionOutput indexing bug, giving comparable recall. Y is materially more precise and disciplined: it correctly flags the still-unhandled len>1 case as an intentional non-issue, cites exact stage_pool.py line numbers, and calibrates severity (non-blocking) matching the actual LGTM outcome, whereas X pads its findings with a shaky unverified claim ('already_submitted is still used later') and mislabels a test-coverage nit as 'major'. X's output is also a messy dump of raw tool state with truncated/duplicated text, badly hurting actionability versus Y's clean, well-organized, line-anchored review.
